segments.text: Lesson 47.

translation.text: 第47课。

segments.text: Too High a Price?

translation.text: 代价是否太高？

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: What does the writer describe as an amusing, old-fashioned source of noise?

translation.text: 作者把什么描述为一种有趣而老式的噪声来源？

segments.text: Pollution is the price we pay for an overpopulated, over-industrialized planet.

translation.text: 污染是我们为人口过剩、工业过度发展的地球付出的代价。

segments.text: When you come to think about it, there are only four ways you can deal with rubbish.

translation.text: 仔细想想，处理垃圾只有四种办法。

segments.text: Dump it, burn it, turn it into something you can use again, attempt to produce less of it.

translation.text: 倾倒、焚烧、回收再利用，或者设法少制造一些。

segments.text: We keep trying all four methods, but the sheer volume of rubbish we produce worldwide threatens to overwhelm us.

translation.text: 我们不断尝试这四种办法，但全球产生的垃圾数量之大，眼看就要把我们淹没。

segments.text: Rubbish, however, is only part of the problem of polluting our planet.

translation.text: 然而，垃圾只是污染地球这一问题的一部分。

segments.text: The need to produce ever-increasing quantities of cheap food leads to a different kind of pollution.

translation.text: 为了生产数量不断增加的廉价食品，又导致了另一种污染。

segments.text: Industrialized farming methods produce cheap meat products—beef, pork, and chicken.

translation.text: 工业化养殖方式生产出廉价肉类——牛肉、猪肉和鸡肉。

segments.text: The use of pesticides and fertilizers produces cheap grain and vegetables.

translation.text: 农药和化肥的使用生产出廉价谷物和蔬菜。

segments.text: The price we pay for cheap food may be already too high.

translation.text: 我们为廉价食品付出的代价或许已经太高。

segments.text: Mad cow disease, BSE in cattle, salmonella in chicken and eggs, and listeria in dairy products.

translation.text: 疯牛病，即牛海绵状脑病；鸡肉和鸡蛋中的沙门氏菌；乳制品中的李斯特菌。

segments.text: And if you think you'll abandon meat and become a vegetarian, you have the choice of very expensive, organically grown vegetables, or a steady diet of pesticides every time you think you're eating fresh salads and vegetables,

translation.text: 如果你想放弃肉食成为素食者，可以选择价格极高的有机蔬菜，或者每次以为自己在吃新鲜沙拉和蔬菜时，都持续摄入农药，

segments.text: or just having an innocent glass of water.

translation.text: 甚至只是喝一杯看似无害的水也不例外。

segments.text: However, there is an even more insidious kind of pollution that particularly affects urban areas and invades our daily lives, and that is noise.

translation.text: 然而，还有一种更加隐蔽的污染，尤其影响城市地区并侵入我们的日常生活，那就是噪声。

segments.text: Burglar alarms going off at any time of the day or night serve only to annoy passers-by and actually assist burglars to burgle.

translation.text: 防盗警报不分昼夜地响，只会惹恼路人，实际上反倒帮助窃贼行窃。

segments.text: alarms constantly scream at us in the street and are a source of profound irritation.

translation.text: 汽车警报器不断在街上冲我们尖叫，成为令人极度烦躁的根源。

segments.text: A recent survey of the effects of noise revealed, surprisingly, that dogs barking incessantly in the night rated the highest form of noise pollution on a scale ranging from one to seven.

translation.text: 最近一项关于噪声影响的调查令人意外地发现，在一至七级的量表上，狗在夜间不停吠叫被列为最严重的噪声污染。

segments.text: The survey revealed a large number of sources of noise that we really dislike.

translation.text: 调查揭示了许多我们实在厌恶的噪声来源。

segments.text: Lawnmowers whining on a summer's day, late-night parties in apartment blocks, noisy neighbors, vehicles of all kinds, especially large container trucks thundering through quiet villages, planes and helicopters flying

translation.text: 夏日里割草机的嗡鸣、公寓楼里的深夜聚会、吵闹的邻居、各种车辆，尤其是轰隆隆穿过安静村庄的大型集装箱卡车，以及从头顶飞过的飞机和直升机，

segments.text: overhead, large radios carried round in public places and played at maximum volume.

translation.text: 还有人们带到公共场所并把音量开到最大的收音机。

segments.text: New technology has also made its own contribution to noise.

translation.text: 新技术也为噪声作出了自己的“贡献”。

segments.text: A lot of people object to mobile phones, especially when they are used in public places like restaurants or on public transport.

translation.text: 许多人反感手机，尤其反感人们在餐馆、公共交通等公共场所使用手机。

segments.text: Loud conversations on mobile phones invade our thoughts or interrupt the pleasure of meeting friends for a quiet chat.

translation.text: 在手机上大声交谈会侵扰我们的思绪，或打断我们同朋友安静聊天的乐趣。

segments.text: noise pollution survey revealed a rather surprising and possibly amusing old-fashioned source of noise.

translation.text: 噪声污染调查还揭示了一种颇出人意料、或许有些好笑的老式噪声来源。

segments.text: It turned out to be snoring.

translation.text: 原来是打鼾。

segments.text: Men were found to be the worst offenders.

translation.text: 调查发现，男人是最严重的制造者。

segments.text: It was revealed that twenty percent of men in their mid-thirties snore.

translation.text: 调查显示，三十五岁左右的男性中有20%打鼾。

segments.text: This figure rises to a staggering sixty percent of men in their sixties.

translation.text: 到六十多岁时，这个数字上升到惊人的60%。

segments.text: Against these figures, it was found that only five percent of women snore regularly, while the rest are constantly woken or kept awake by their trumpeting partners.

translation.text: 与这些数字相比，只有5%的女性经常打鼾，其余女性则不断被她们鼾声如号角的伴侣吵醒或弄得无法入睡。

segments.text: Whatever the source of noise, one thing is certain.

translation.text: 无论噪声来自哪里，有一点是肯定的。

segments.text: Silence, it seems, has become a golden memory.

translation.text: 看来，寂静已经成了珍贵的回忆。
